member.name: Ian Lavery

addresses: 7 Esther Court, Wansbeck Business park, Ashington, Northumberland, NE63 8AP

member.synopsis: Ian Lavery is the Labour MP for Blyth and Ashington , and has been an MP continually since 6 May 2010.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.

writtenQuestions.heading: Skilled Workers: Vacancies
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department is taking to help tackle skills shortages in areas historically impacted by deindustrialisation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are strengthening the skills system so people of all ages can gain the qualifications needed for jobs in a changing economy.Skills England leads on work to identify skills needs, simplify the system, and align training with labour‑market demand. It als...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: British Business Bank
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that investment from the British Business Bank is spread evenly throughout the country.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The British Business Bank seeks to improve access to finance for businesses across the UK through both national and regional interventions. The former includes the Start Up Loans scheme, where loans have been issued in every UK parliamentary constituency,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Economic Growth
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what steps her Department is taking to encourage a culture of economic growth in deindustrialised areas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to improving economic growth and living standards across every part of the United Kingdom, including in communities affected by the long-term decline of industry. We are moving away from a model where growth is concentrated in ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Unemployment: Investment

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to improving economic growth and living standards across every part of the United Kingdom, including in communities affected by the long-term decline of industry. We are moving away from a model where growth is concentrated in ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Business: Disadvantaged
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department has taken to encourage businesses in deprived areas historically impacted by deindustrialisation to employ people locally.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Business and Trade is committed to supporting economic growth and job creation across all parts of the United Kingdom, including in communities historically affected by deindustrialisation. The key objective of the Industrial Strategy i...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Northern Powerhouse Investment Fund
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what the Northern Powerhouse investment fund has done to increase employment in areas historically impacted by deindustrialisation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The two generations of the Northern Powerhouse Investment Fund (NPIF) support employment across the North of England by increasing access to finance for smaller businesses to start, scale and innovate.The first NPIF delivered more than £1 billion of combi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Growth Hubs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that support offered by Growth Hubs is not only used in places of concentrated wealth.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Growth Hub network comprises 38 local business support bodies delivered by or via local authorities in England. DBT provides funding to authorities for their Growth Hubs to deliver a core service and to act as local delivery partners for the new Busin...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Media: Mental Health

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
